summary: In cases where traditional approaches fail due to severe jaw displacements from mandibular fractures, submental intubation serves effectively as a viable substitute for tracheostomy ensuring safe ventilation without compromising patient stability during critical care management phases involving maxillofacial trauma scenarios. The technique addresses specific challenges associated with anatomical deformities caused by these types of fractures making it indispensable when other routes cannot be utilized safely or efficiently
